span.p:
Does a one-time item sit in one side
of the comparison only?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
ISOLATE THE ONE-TIME first
restate run-rate, then decompose the rest

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Does the line consolidate FX-denominated
subs AND the rate moved > 2pct?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
CONSTANT-CURRENCY SPLIT first
FX effect vs operating variance

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Is the line revenue, COGS, or a
unit-driven cost with multiple products?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
PRICE / VOLUME / MIX bridge
three effects that sum to total

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
RATE x VOLUME two-way bridge
mix folded into rate (state it)

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
NAME THE DRIVER directly
decision / accrual / timing — no bridge
